column1: What are the six stages of the Lead-to-Cash sales process in order?
column2: Master Data, Presales Activities, Order Processing, Product Delivery, Invoicing, Money

column1: In SAP Sales, what three elements combine to form a "Sales Area"?
column2: Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, and Division

column1: How many Company Codes can a single Sales Organization be assigned to?
What is the highest-level organizational unit responsible for the entire shipping process?
At what organizational level is a "Delivery Plant" assigned?
column2: The combination of Sales Organization and Distribution Channel

column1: What Business Partner role is required specifically for Sales Area data (shipping, billing)?
Which Business Partner role contains the reconciliation account for the General Ledger?
Why is product master data extended "locally" to Sales Organizations and Plants?
column2: To maintain specific settings for pricing, packaging, and transport that vary by location

column1: Which material type (ID) is used for a "Finished Product" in SAP?
What four elements are typically included in Condition Master data?
What is the purpose of Output Master Data in the sales process?
column2: To define how information (like order confirmations) is sent to the customer (e.g., PDF, EDI)

column1: Which SAP system provides a 360-degree view of customer interactions for field sales teams?
In the SAP sales cycle, what is a "Lead"?
column2: A potential sales contact (individual or organization) generated from marketing or referrals

column1: What sales document collects estimated deal value, products, and sales probability?
What is "Guided Selling" in the context of an Opportunity?
column2: A feature that walks the sales team through specific tasks required to create a quote

column1: When a Sales Quote is accepted and converted to an order, where is the order fulfilled?
What check is automatically triggered in a sales order to see if stock exists for a requested date?
In Partner Determination, who is the "Sold-to party"?
column2: The entity that places the order

column1: In Partner Determination, who is the "Payer"?
column2: The entity responsible for paying the invoice

column1: What is the function of an "Incompletion Log" in a sales order?
column2: To ensure all mandatory data is entered before the document can proceed to delivery

column1: What are the three levels of a sales document structure?
Which level of a sales document contains the confirmed delivery quantities and dates?
What is the primary reference document used to create an Outbound Delivery?
What must be true regarding the "Pick Quantity" before a Goods Issue can be posted?
column2: The Pick Quantity must equal the Delivery Quantity

column1: What accounting update occurs to inventory valuation when a Goods Issue is posted?
column2: Inventory valuation decreases (Debit COGS, Credit Inventory)

column1: Which document serves as the data source for Financial Accounting to monitor customer payments?
What two postings occur in the General Ledger when an invoice is saved?
column2: Debit Customer Receivables and Credit Revenue

column1: What SAP tool allows you to see the chronological history and status of all linked sales documents?
What is the accounting entry for an incoming payment from a customer?
column2: Debit Cash/Bank account and Credit Customer Receivables

column1: What is the benefit of using an "Electronic Bank Statement" in the Money phase?
column2: It allows the system to automatically import files and reconcile incoming payments

column1: In the Lead-to-Cash process, which two document types are categorized as "Presales"?
Which organizational level is specifically required to create a delivery document?
In Financial Accounting, what is the debit/credit effect on the Cash account when an incoming payment is posted?
column2: The Cash account is debited (increasing the asset)

column1: In SAP EWM, which specific action is required to finalize the "Outbound Delivery" status?
Which specific type of master data controls the calculation of customer discounts and surcharges?
What must be done with Electronic Bank Statement items that the system fails to reconcile automatically?
During Sales Order processing, which feature automatically verifies if a product can be delivered by the requested date?
Aside from the Billing Document, which specific step in the shipping process triggers the creation of a Financial Accounting (FI) document?
What are the three core sub-processes involved in the Outbound Delivery phase?
column2: Picking, Packing, and Posting Goods Issue

column1: What is the purpose of the "Availability Check" during Sales Order processing?
column2: To ensure the requested material is in stock or will be available by the delivery date

column1: In the context of sales pricing, what are three common classifications of "Conditions"?
What is the relationship between "Picking" and "Posting Goods Issue"?
column2: The Pick Quantity must equal the Delivery Quantity before the Goods Issue can be posted
